content.full_text:
State of California
A-77-247

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 9, 1977

John D. Nolan
City Administrator
1735 Montgomery Street
Oroville, California 95965

Re: Conflict of Interest Code
for Designated Employees
of the City of Oroville

Dear Mr. Nolan:

On or about January 11, 1977, we received at this office a copy of Resolution #3227 adopting a Conflict of Interest Code applicable to designated employees of the City of Oroville for which the City Council is the code reviewing body. As the code reviewing body pursuant to Government Code Section 87303 possesses the authority to adopt or revise a proposed Conflict of Interest Code. Each Conflict of Interest Code must contain certain mandatory provisions as required by Government Code Section 87300 et seq.

One of the Fair Political Practices Commission's duties, contained in Government Code Section 83113(c), requires that the Commission, "Provide assistance to agencies and public officials in administering the provisions of this title." Accordingly, I am enclosing the following suggestions with respect to certain provisions contained in your Code, as adopted.

It appears that your Code, as adopted, does not meet the requirements of Government Code Section 87309 which provides:

"87309. Requirements for Approval. No Conflict of Interest Code or amendment shall be approved by the code reviewing body or upheld by a court if it:

(a) Fails to provide reasonable assurance that all foreseeable potential conflict of interest situations will be disclosed or prevented;

(b) Fails to provide to each affected person a clear and specific statement of his duties under the Code; or

(c) Fails to adequately differentiate between designated employees with different powers and responsibilities."

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322-5501 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

John D. Nolan
June 9, 1977
page two

The Commission has consistently held in its deliberations that Codes
which do not adequately differentiate between designated employees are not
acceptable pursuant to 87309(c), above. Further, that such lack of differ-
entiation is the type of defect disfavored by 87309(b). The real danger
in such a lack of differentiation is that it results in overly broad
disclosure categories which could probably not withstand a legal challenge.

Use of Government Code Section 87310 for the purpose of avoiding the
specificity required in devising disclosure categories is clearly not in
keeping with the intent of Section 87310.

Please note that Section 87310 provides:

"87310. Designated Employee; Broad or Indefinable Duties.
If the duties of a designated employee are so broad or
indefinable that the requirements of Section 87309 cannot
be complied with, the Conflict of Interest Code shall require
the designated employee to comply with the requirements of
Article 2 of this chapter."

Section 87310 is prefaced with the uncertain term "if" which probably
means, especially when read in context with 87302(a) and 87309, if and
only if, an employee's duties are so broad as to defy an attempt to define
the person's responsibilities and the types of interests they could affect,
then and only then may 87310 be used to require Article 2 disclosure.

The other oversight contained in your Code is a failure to provide for
a "leaving office statement"; this is understandable since the requirement
of a leaving office statement is not expressly mentioned in Government Code
Section 87300 et seq. The Commission, however, as the interpreter of the
Act has determined that leaving office statements are mandatory. (See
Commission memorandum dated August 25, 1976 attached)

If you have any further questions regarding the above, please call me
at 916/322-6444.

Sincerely,

Alfredo Flores
Research Specialist
Conflicts of Interest Division

AF:ma
Attachment

1735 MONTGOMERY STREET                    CROVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95965  AREA CODE 916  833-6551

OFFICE OF THE
CITY ADMINISTRATOR - CITY CLERK

January 6, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Enclosed is certified copy of Resolution No. 3227
adopting a conflict of interest code applicable to
certain designated employees.

If you have any questions about this conflict of
interest code, please contact me.

Sincerely,

John D. Nolan
City Administrator

JDN:ef

Enc.
